0.text: I, Omni, was commanded by my father Jarom to write something on these plates to preserve our genealogy.

In my days, I want you to know that I fought many battles with the sword to protect my people, the Nephites, from falling to their enemies, the Lamanites. But I myself am a wicked man, and I have not kept the statutes and commandments of the Lord as I should have.

Two hundred and seventy-six years had passed, and we had many seasons of peace and many seasons of serious war and bloodshed. In total, two hundred and eighty-two years had passed. I kept these plates according to my fathers' commandments and gave them to my son Amaron. I make an end.
Now I, Amaron, write the few things I write in the book of my father.
Three hundred and twenty years had passed, and the more wicked part of the Nephites were destroyed.

The Lord would not allow — after leading them out of Jerusalem and protecting them from their enemies — his words to go unfulfilled. He had told our fathers, "If you will not keep my commandments, you will not prosper in the land."
The Lord visited them with severe judgment. Yet he spared the righteous so they would not be destroyed, delivering them from the hands of their enemies.
I gave the plates to my brother Chemish.

Now I, Chemish, write the few things I write in the same book as my brother. I saw the last thing he wrote — he wrote it with his own hand on the day he gave the plates to me. This is how we keep the records, according to our fathers' commandments. I make an end.
I, Abinadom, am the son of Chemish. I saw much war and conflict between my people, the Nephites, and the Lamanites. With my own sword, I took the lives of many Lamanites in defense of my people.

The record of this people is engraved on plates kept by the kings, according to the generations. I know of no revelation or prophecy beyond what has already been written. What has been written is sufficient. I make an end.

I am Amaleki, the son of Abinadom. I will tell you about Mosiah, who was made king over the land of Zarahemla. He was warned by the Lord to flee from the land of Nephi. As many as would listen to the voice of the Lord also left the land with him into the wilderness.

He did as the Lord commanded. They departed into the wilderness — as many as would listen to the Lord. They were led by many preachings and prophesyings. They were continually guided by the word of God and led by the power of his arm through the wilderness until they came to a land called Zarahemla.

They discovered a people called the people of Zarahemla. There was great rejoicing among both groups. Zarahemla rejoiced greatly because the Lord had sent the people of Mosiah with the brass plates containing the record of the Jews.
Mosiah discovered that the people of Zarahemla had come from Jerusalem at the time Zedekiah, king of Judah, was carried away captive to Babylon.
They had traveled through the wilderness and been brought by the Lord's hand across the great waters to the land where Mosiah found them. They had lived there ever since.

By the time Mosiah found them, they had become extremely numerous. However, they had experienced many wars and serious conflicts. Many had fallen by the sword over time. Their language had become corrupted. They brought no records with them, and they denied the existence of their Creator. Mosiah and his people could not understand them.
But Mosiah had them taught his language. After they learned Mosiah's language, Zarahemla gave a genealogy of his ancestors from memory. This genealogy is written down, but not on these plates.
The people of Zarahemla and the people of Mosiah united together, and Mosiah was appointed to be their king.
During the days of Mosiah, a large stone was brought to him with engravings on it. He interpreted the engravings by the gift and power of God.
The engravings told about a man named Coriantumr and the destruction of his people. Coriantumr had been found by the people of Zarahemla, and he lived among them for nine months.

The stone also mentioned a few things about Coriantumr's ancestors. His first ancestors came from the great tower at the time the Lord confused the languages of the people. The severity of the Lord fell upon them according to his just judgments. Their bones lay scattered in the land to the north.
I, Amaleki, was born during the days of Mosiah, and I lived to see his death. Benjamin, his son, reigns in his place.

In the days of King Benjamin, I saw a serious war and much bloodshed between the Nephites and the Lamanites. But the Nephites gained great advantage over them — so much that King Benjamin drove them out of the land of Zarahemla.

I began to grow old. Having no children, and knowing King Benjamin to be a just man before the Lord, I will give these plates to him. I urge all people to come to God, the Holy One of Israel, and believe in prophesying, revelations, the ministering of angels, the gift of speaking in tongues, the gift of interpreting languages, and all things that are good. Nothing is good unless it comes from the Lord, and what is evil comes from the devil.

Now, my beloved brothers and sisters, I want you to come to Christ, who is the Holy One of Israel, and partake of his salvation and the power of his redemption. Come to him and offer your whole souls as an offering. Continue in fasting and praying, and endure to the end. As the Lord lives, you will be saved.
I will mention a group who went up into the wilderness to return to the land of Nephi. A large number of people wanted to possess the land of their ancestors.
They went up into the wilderness. Their leader was a strong, mighty, and stubborn man who caused conflict among them. All of them were killed except fifty, who returned to the land of Zarahemla.
They took a considerable number of people with them and set out into the wilderness again.
I, Amaleki, had a brother who went with them. I have heard nothing about them since. I am about to go to my grave. These plates are full. I make an end of my speaking.
